Name:
Been Around The World [V1]
(prod. Sunny J Beats)
Notes:
OG Filename: rockyasap1.jpg
Black and white photo of A$AP Rocky taken by Mikey B Lyke used in the OnSmash.com article by LEGEND about Rocky's then newly released song "Been Around The World". The photo comes from a early May 2010 photoshoot, with this same photo being uploaded to Mikey B Lyke's now deleted Tumblr page on May 6th, 2010. Another photo from this photoshoot was used on multiple videos uploaded to A$AP Yams' HarlemzMostHated YouTube channel from May to October 2010.

Name:
HarlemzMostHated [YouTube Thumbnails]
(Uptown, Bittersweet Symphony Part 2, Been Around The World (Slow Lane Remix)
Notes: Another photo black and white photo of A$AP Rocky taken by Mikey B Lyke in a ~May 2010 photoshoot that was used in multiple thumbnails by A$AP Yams for his HarlemzMostHated YouTube channel where he uploaded some 'unreleased' songs by Rocky as a way to get recognition throughout May to October 2010. This photo was used in the uploads for "Uptown" on May 2010, "Been Around The World (Slow Lane Remix)" on October 20th, 2010 and "Bittersweet Symphony" on October 23rd, 2010. It is possible other songs were uploaded to the channel with this photo but it has been wiped from YouTube and the Wayback Machine.

Name:
Houston Old Head [V1]
(prod. DJ Burn One)
Notes: Cover art attached to the file for the original version of "Houston Old Head" uploaded to A$AP Yams' then famous and now deleted Tumblr page 'realniggatumblr' on November 1st, 2010. The cover features rather poor graphic design with an otherwise unseen 'A$AP ROCKY' logo with the title in 3D text. The background of the cover is a reference to lean as it shows jolly ranchers and cough syrup, which matches the purple vibe of the art.

Name:
Been Around The World [V2/Tumblr Release]
(prod. Sunny J Beats)
Notes: Cover attached to the file of the reupload/rerelease of the song "Been Around The World" that was uploaded to A$AP Yams' then famous and now deleted Tumblr page 'realniggatumblr' on December 8th, 2010. The cover shows a low-quality still from the "Nuthin' But A "G" Thang' music video by Dr. Dre of a man revealing a woman's nude breasts with the text "ASAP ROCKY" and "BEEN AROUND THE WORLD".

Name:
Me, Myself & A$AP
(Me, Myself And ASAP)

Purple Swag [V1/YouTube Thumbnail]
(prod. A$AP Ty Beats)
Notes: Selfie taken by A$AP Rocky in what seems to be the backseat of a vehicle that was used as the YouTube thumbnail for the original release of the song "Purple Swag" on A$AP Yams' 'HarlemzMostHated' channel on April 14th, 2011.

Name: Deep Purple
Notes: Cover for the unofficial compilation "Deep Purple" before Rocky blew up. Because of this Rocky promoted the compilation and even had it as his FaceBook profile picture in 2011, giving it some form of legitimacy.

Name: Mouth Fulla Gold
Notes: Cover art for the unreleased mixtape "Mouth Fulla Gold" that appears on all of the files metadata on the mixtape. The cover features a photo of Rocky with a filter that makes the picture have a slight gold tint.

Name:
A$AP Ferg - Meagan Good
(feat. A$AP Rocky) (prod. Perry Eats)
Notes: Single art for the song "Meagan Good" was shown by A$AP Ferg on his Tumblr page two days after the song released on June 6th, 2011.

Name: Wassup
Notes: Cover art used for the original upload of Wassup on Clams Casino's Soundcloud account which would later be deleted. This exact image was also posted onto Rocky's official Facebook on July 7th 2011.

Name:
Peso
(feat. Kevin Andreas) (prod. A$AP Ty Beats)
Notes: The single art for the song "Peso" which was released on July 21st, 2011 using an image taken during Brock Fetch's 2011 photoshoots with the A$AP Mob. This cover is very similar to the back cover to "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP", but this version of the photo edits out the people surrounding Rocky and the couch he is sitting on, along with the flag being a different color purple.

Name:
Smoke DZA - 4 Loko
(feat. A$AP Rocky) (prod. 183rd)
Notes: Single art for the song "4 Loko" by Smoke DZA featuring Rocky which was released on August 18th, 2011. The cover features a distorted picture of the two artists together with a purple tint on the whole picture.

Name:
Main Attrakionz - U.G.K.
(feat. A$AP Rocky) (prod. CashFlow)
Notes: One of the cover arts for the Main Attrakionz & A$AP Rocky collaboration titled "U/G.K.". The cover is just simply text that says "Ballers Eve" which is where the track was posted.

Name:
Main Attrakionz - U.G.K. [Alternate Cover 1]
(feat. A$AP Rocky) (prod. CashFlow)
Notes: One of the cover arts for the Main Attrakionz & A$AP Rocky collaboration titled "U/G.K.". The cover is just simply text that says "Ballers Eve" which is where the track was posted but has different aspect ratio compared to the other cover.

Name:
Main Attrakionz - U.G.K. [Alternate Cover 2]
(feat. A$AP Rocky) (prod. CashFlow)
Notes: One of the cover arts for the Main Attrakionz & A$AP Rocky collaboration titled "U/G.K.". The cover is just simply text that says "Ballers Eve" which is where the track was posted but has different aspect ratio compared to the other cover.

Name:
Purple Swag Bootleg
(with Jim-E Stack) (prod. A$AP Ty Beats & Jim-E Stack)
Notes: Official cover art used for the "Purple Swag" bootleg remix created by A$AP Rocky and producer Jim-E Stack.

Name:
Seth Narley - Grippin' Woodgrain
(feat. A$AP Rocky) (prod. Epik)
Notes: The single art for the song "Grippin' Grainwood" by Seth Narley featuring Rocky which was released on October 26th, 2011. The cover features a low quality image of a car with text that has a wood texture. The songs title is listed as "Grippin' Grainwood" on the official release, but the cover has the name of "Gripping Grainwood".

Name:
Seth Narley - Grippin' Woodgrain [Alternative Cover 2]
(feat. A$AP Rocky) (prod. Epik)
Notes: Second alternative cover art used for the SoundCloud upload of Grippin' Woodgrain featuring Rocky. Is simply just an image of Seth Narley which is very 2011 esque.

Name: LIVE.LOVE.A$AP
Notes: The original cover for A$AP Rocky's 2011 mixtape "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP" that was released on October 31st, 2011. The cover is not a perfect square but features a photo taken by Brock Fetch during him and the A$AP Mob's 2011 photoshoots and is a nod to Outkast's 2000 album "Stanktonia" which Rocky revealed in 2012 that it is one of his favorite albums. This cover would later be referenced in his first studio album "LONG.LIVE.A$AP" with an American flag being wrapped around Rocky and was also referenced in the cover of his second studio album "AT.LONG.LAST.A$AP" with a small American flag.

Name: LIVE.LOVE.A$AP [Alternative Cover]
Notes: Alternative cover art for LIVE.LOVE.A$AP posted by Kelvin Krash on his website when the mixtape re-release came out. Features another photo taken by Brock Fetch during him and the A$AP Mob's 2011 photoshoots.

Name: LIVE.LOVE.A$AP [Back Cover]
Notes: The back cover for Rocky's mixtape "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP" that was released on October 31st, 2011. The cover shares similarities to the "Peso" single art, except in that cover there is no one surrounding Rocky and the couch and the flag is a different color of purple. Notably the song "Acid Drip" is alternatively titled "Acid Drips" in the tracklist surrounding the photo for unknown reasons along with the song "Houston Old Head" not having it's producer next to it as all of the other songs in the photo do.

Name:
Purple Swag
(prod. A$AP Ty Beats)
Notes: Single art for the song "Purple Swag" that was eventually released on December 5th, 2011 although the song was originally released on April 14th, 2011. The cover features a photo taken by Brock Fetch from his 2011 photoshoots with the A$AP Mob with the smoke coming from Rocky's mouth being edited to the color purple.

Name:
Lord$ Never Worry
(Collaboration with A$AP Mob)
(TRILLUMINATI, LONG.LIVE.A$AP)

Bath Salt
(perf. A$AP Rocky, A$AP Ant & Flatbush Zombies) (prod. A$AP P on the Boards)
Notes: The main single art for the song "Bath Salt" that was released on July 27th, 2012. The cover features a casket which has the featured artists' names appearing. The name YG Addie refers to A$AP Ant, Pretty Flacko refers to Rocky himself, and the Flatbush Zombies did not go under a nickname as the others did. This version of the cover has a darker shade of black in the background and has the "assassinate snitches and police" in the four corners blacked out but slightly still visible.

Name:
Bath Salt [Alternate Cover]
(perf. A$AP Rocky, A$AP Ant & Flatbush Zombies) (prod. A$AP P on the Boards)
Notes: An alternate cover for the song "Bath Salt" that was released on July 27th, 2012. This is a more of an original cover as the acronym for A$AP, "assassinate snitches and police", in the four corners are not blacked out as the main cover has. Along with this the cover is more zoomed out making the casket and song title smaller. Along with this the cover features a lighter shade of black in the background.

Name: Lord$ Never Worry [Promotional Image]
Notes: A promotional imagine for the A$AP Mob's mixtape "Lord$ Never Worry" that was shared on asapmob.com on August 11th, 2012. The imagine shows a black and white American flag with the the release date of the mixtape, August 28th, 2012, under the title.

Name:
Work
(with A$AP Ferg) (prod. Chinza//Fly)
Notes: The single art for the song "Work" with A$AP Ferg that was released on August 20th, 2012. This cover features a photo from a photoshoot A$AP Ferg had in 2012 at some sort of abandoned warehouse. The photo from the cover was reused in a early cover for his album "Trap Lord" with the picture being the background of the text with a white background.

Name: Lord$ Never Worry
Notes: The main cover art for A$AP Mob's mixtape "Lord$ Never Worry" that was released on August 28th, 2012 which pictures the main members of the A$AP Mob at the time all pictured together. The members featured starting from the top row left to right are A$AP Yams, A$AP Rocky, A$AP Nast and A$AP Twelvyy. The last three members seen in the bottom row are A$AP Ty Beats, A$AP Ferg and A$AP Ant. The members seen all have matching jerseys on which on the back have their names or their nicknames with the numbers "06" referring to the year the A$AP Mob was created, in 2006.

Name: Lord$ Never Worry [Animated Cover]
Notes: An animated version of the "Lord$ Never Worry" cover which was released on August 28th, 2012. The only notable difference in this animated cover is the flame animation plastered on the whole cover.

Name: Lord$ Never Worry [Back Cover]
Notes: A very basic back cover for the A$AP Mob's mixtape "Lord$ Never Worry" which was released in August 28th, 2012. The cover features the tracklist which features all of the producers of the songs. Along with this, the logo got the website livemixtapes.com can be seen as that is where the mixtape was officially released.

Name:
Smoke DZA - 4 Loko (Remix)
(feat. A$AP Rocky, A$AP Twelvyy, Killa Kyleon, Danny Brown & Freeway) (prod. 183rd)
Notes: The cover art for the remix of Smoke DZA's song "4 Loko" which was released on December 24th, 2011. The cover simply features photos of each of the artists. From left to right starting at the top there is Smoke DZA, Rocky, A$AP Twelvyy, Danny Brown, Killa Kyleon and then Freeway. All of the photos were edited to be black and white as well.

Name:
Pretty Flacko
(prod. SpaceGhostPurrp)
Notes: The single art for the song titled "Pretty Flacko" which was released on January 1st, 2012. The cover very simplify takes a photo of Rocky that was originally black and white and gave it a green and purple tint. The cover also has the producer of the song at the bottom along with a square that has Rocky's name and the title of the song that covers his eyes.

Name:
ScHoolboy Q - Hands On The Wheel
(feat. A$AP Rocky) (prod. Best Kept Secret)
Notes: Single art for ScHoolboy Q's song "Hands On The Wheel" which features Rocky that was released on January 14th, 2012. The cover is very simple as it features a gold colored signature of ScHoolboy Q's name. The only detail to note is the "On The" in the title is a different font than "Hands" and "Wheel" with this font being more of a handwritten style.

Name:
David Banner - Yao Ming (Remix)
(feat. A$AP Rocky & Chris Brown) (prod. David Banner & Swiff D)
Notes: The single art for the remix of David Banner's song "Yao Ming" which was was released on February 3rd, 2012. The cover shows a depiction of the NBA player Yao Ming as that is who the song is named after. The cover has the artists names at the bottom along with the Chinese flag in the background.

Name:
David Banner - Yao Ming (Remix)
(feat. A$AP Rocky & Chris Brown) (prod. David Banner & Swiff D)
Notes: Album art for David Banner's 2012 project Sex, Drugs & Video Games, Art is in the style of the video game Grand Theft Auto and features all of the projects features in the back.

Name: Live Love Purple
Notes: Cover art for Live Love Purple which is a chopped and screwed version of the 2011 mixtape hosted by OG Ron C, DJ Candlestick, DJ Mr. Rogers & Chopstars

Name:
Swizz Beatz - Street Knock
(feat. A$AP Rocky) (prod. araabMUZIK & Swizz Beatz)
Notes: The original single art for Swizz Beatz' song "Street Knock" which features Rocky that was released on March 23rd, 2012. The cover shows Swizz Beatz yelling into the microphone with a Reebok hat on.

Name:
Theophilus London - Big Spender
(feat. A$AP Rocky) (prod. Gordo)
Notes: Single art for Theophilus London's song "Big Spender" that features Rocky which was released on April 15th, 2012. This cover is animated and has a glittering effect on the diamonds.

Name:
Goldie
(prod. Hit-Boy)
Notes: The main single art for the song "Goldie" which was released on April 16th, 2012. The cover features a lot more color than the rest of the singles from the "LONG.LIVE.A$AP" album. While the photo is black and white the grills Rocky was wearing have their actual gold color to it.

Name:
Goldie [Alternate Cover]
(prod. Hit-Boy)
Notes: An alternate black and white single art for the song "Goldie" which was released on April 16th, 2012. This cover more correlates with the other single covers for "LONG.LIVE.A$AP", but does not completely match the style. The cover states the producer of the track, Hit-Boy.

Name:
Swizz Beatz - Street Knock [Alternate Streaming Cover] 
(feat. A$AP Rocky) (prod. araabMUZIK & Swizz Beatz)
Notes: The cover art for Swizz Beatz' song "Street Knock" that appears on streaming services which was related on May 8th, 2012. The cover shows a mouth open wide with grills on the persons teeth with the tracks name and artists inside. The cover does not state the producer of the song as the other cover does.

Name:
Rihanna - Cockiness (I Love It) [Remix]
(feat. A$AP Rocky) (prod. Kuk Harrell & 
Bangladesh)
Notes: Single art for the remix to Rihanna's song "Cockiness (I Love It)" which was released on September 5th, 2012. The cover art features a newspaper as the background with Rihanna's "R" logo. More specifically this logo similar to the version that was used on the "Talk That Talk" album cover but is created by a bunch of seemingly random words all with a blue color.

Name:
Fuckin' Problems
(feat. Drake, Kendrick Lamar & 2 Chainz) (prod. 40 & Drake)
Notes: The single art for the song titled "Fuckin' Problems" featuring Drake, 2 Chainz and Kendrick Lamar which was released on October 24th, 2012.  The cover features a 2012 photo taken under a bridge in Harlem, New York. The same image here was used in a semi-popular unofficial mixtape "Forever ASAP".

Name:
Fat Joe - Yellow Tape
(feat. A$AP Rocky, Lil Wayne, French Montana & DJ Khaled) (prod. Infared & IllaDaProducer)
Notes: Cover art for the Fat Joe's single "Yellow Tape" which was released on October 30th, 2012. The cover very simply features yellow tape with the song name and featured artists.

Name: LONG.LIVE.A$AP
Notes:
OG Filename: FINAL_STANDARD
The main cover art for the standard edition of Rocky's first studio album titled "LONG.LIVE.A$AP" was revealed on December 3rd, 2012, but was released on January 15th, 2013. The cover was designed by Joe Perez with a photo taken by Phil Knott shows Rocky wrapping an American flag around his body which is a call back to Rocky's first mixtape "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP" while a flag would appear again on "AT.LONG.LAST.A$AP". This version of the cover has Rocky's lower half on top with his upper half on the bottom which is not the case on the deluxe versions of the cover. Similarly to the first version of the the deluxe cover the coloration is different making Rocky's skin noticeably darker than the final deluxe cover. i

Name: LONG.LIVE.A$AP (Deluxe) [V1]
Notes: Original version of the "LONG.LIVE.A$AP" deluxe cover which was shown off for the pre-order of the album on December 11th, 2012. This cover is similar to the standard edition version as the coloration is different making Rocky's skin noticeably darker than in the other deluxe cover. This version shows the complete picture of Rocky rather than him being split.

Name:
Long Live A$AP
(prod. A$AP Rocky, FNZ, Jim Jonsin, Rico Love & Frank Romano)
Notes: The main single art for the song "Long Live A$AP", the title track to Rocky's first studio album "LONG.LIVE.A$AP", which was released on December 18th, 2012. The cover was designed by Joe Perez features Rocky sitting on a throne with thunder in the background. It shows similarities to the "Wild for the Night" single art with the thunder, and one of the "Fashion Killa" single covers which also features a throne.

Name:
Long Live A$AP [Animated Cover]
(prod. A$AP Rocky, FNZ, Jim Jonsin, Rico Love & Frank Romano)
Notes: An animated version of the "Long Live A$AP" single cover which was released on December 18th, 2012. The cover which was designed by Joe Perez is pretty much identical to the main one other than the obvious featured animated effects.

Name: LONG.LIVE.A$AP (Deluxe) [Back Cover]
Notes: Back cover for the deluxe edition of "LONG.LIVE.A$AP" that was shown off on December 23rd, 2012. The back cover features the tracklist for the album along with added the bonus tracks, as well as the producers of all of the songs as some of the other back covers do not.

Name:
Wild for the Night
(with Skrillex & Birdy Nam Nam) (prod. Skrillex, A$AP Rocky, Birdy Nam Nam)
Notes: The single art for the song titled "Wild for the Night" with Skrillex and Birdy Nam Nam which was released with this cover on SoundCloud on January 12th, 2013, but later released as a single on March 26th, 2013. The cover which was designed by Joe Perez shows Rocky standing with three people with skulls as heads. The cover is very similar to the "Long Live A$AP" and "Fashion Killa" single arts.

Name:
Pretty Flacko (Remix)
(feat. Waka Flocka Flame, Gucci Mane & Pharrell Williams) (prod. SpaceGhostPurrp)
Notes: Due to the beef between Rocky and SpaceGhostPurrp the song "Pretty Flacko (Remix)", which was originally intended to appear on the album, was instead included with the the purchase of "LONG.LIVE.A$AP" on January 15th, 2013. The covers original imagine is a photo of Rocky sipping on a cup of whine while wearing a hat that states "VSVP". The cover colors his ring gold as well which makes it stand out amongst other covers.

Name: LONG.LIVE.A$AP (Deluxe) [V2]
Notes: Updated deluxe cover for Rocky's first studio album "LONG.LIVE.A$AP" which was released on January 15th, 2013 and overtime has became the 'main' album cover even though this is just for the deluxe. The cover was designed by Joe Perez with a photo taken by Phil Knott shows Rocky wrapping an American flag around his body which is a call back to Rocky's first mixtape "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP" while a flag would appear again on "AT.LONG.LAST.A$AP". The difference from the original cover is that this is the full image of Rocky rather than him being split, but this cover is different from both versions as the coloration is different making Rocky's skin noticeably brighter.

Name: LONG.LIVE.A$AP [CD Back Cover]
Notes: Back cover for the CD version of the album "LONG.LIVE.A$AP" which was released on January 15th, 2013. The cover features the tracklist for the album but does not list the producer for each track as the deluxe pre-order tracklist did. The bottom of the cover features a lot of information along with several of Rocky's social medias and websites.

Name: LONG.LIVE.A$AP (Deluxe) [Vinyl Back Cover]
Notes: Back cover for the vinyl version of the album "LONG.LIVE.A$AP" which was released on January 15th, 2013. The cover features the tracklist for the album but does not list the producer for each track as the deluxe pre-order tracklist did. The bottom of the cover features a lot of information along with several of Rocky's social medias and websites. The cover also features sides one through four all featuring four tracks each.

Name: LONG.LIVE.A$AP [CD Showcase #1]
Notes: Frontside of the CD packaging for LONG.LIVE.A$AP, posted on Joe Perez's portfolio.

Name: LONG.LIVE.A$AP [CD Showcase #2]
Notes: Backside of the CD packaging for LONG.LIVE.A$AP, posted on Joe Perez's portfolio.

Name: LONG.LIVE.A$AP (Deluxe) [CD Disc Tray]
Notes: The CD disc tray for the album "LONG.LIVE.A$AP" which was released on January 15th, 2013. The disc that was designed by Joe Perez has the static effect with Rocky and the albums name along with credits at the bottom.

Name: LONG.LIVE.A$AP (Deluxe) [CD Booklet Front & Back Cover]
Notes: The cover for the "LONG.LIVE.A$AP" CD booklet which was released on January 15th, 2013 and shared by the designer Joe Perez himself. Features the main cover on the front and a continuation of it for the back half.

Name: LONG.LIVE.A$AP (Deluxe) [CD Booklet Page #1 & #2]
Notes: The first two pages to the CD booklet for the "LONG.LIVE.A$AP" album which was released on January 15th, 2013 shared by the designer Joe Perez himself. This is the first tracklist page featuring the songs "Long Live A$AP", "Goldie" and "PMW (All I Really Need)" while also stating each tracks credits. The second page also shows the tracklist featuring the songs "LVL", "Hell" and "Pain" also stating the credits for each track. The pages along with the rest of the booklet follow the style of government documents.

Name: LONG.LIVE.A$AP (Deluxe) [CD Booklet Page #3 & #4]
Notes: The third and fourth pages to the CD booklet for the "LONG.LIVE.A$AP" album which was released on January 15th, 2013 shared by the designer Joe Perez himself. The third page shows an ID of Rocky with his name listed as A$AP Rocky, his preferred name as LONG.LIVE.A$AP, his real birthday as well as the issue date being June 9th, 2012, but it is unknown what significance this date holds as of now. The fourth page in the booklet continues the tracklist featuring the songs "F***kin' Problems" and "Wild for the Night" while also stating the credits for the songs. The pages along with the rest of the booklet follow the style of government documents.

Name: LONG.LIVE.A$AP (Deluxe) [CD Booklet Page #5 & #6]
Notes: The fifth and sixth pages to the CD booklet for the "LONG.LIVE.A$AP" album which was released on January 15th, 2013 shared by the designer Joe Perez himself. The fifth page continues the tracklists featuring the songs "1Train" and "Fashion Killa" while stating the credits for both songs. The sixth page also continues the tracklist featuring "Phoenix", "Suddenly" and lists the first of the bonus tracks "Jodye" while staying the credits for each of those songs. The pages along with the rest of the booklet follow the style of government documents.

Name: LONG.LIVE.A$AP (Deluxe) [CD Booklet Page #7 & #8]
Notes: The sixth and seventh pages to the CD booklet for the "LONG.LIVE.A$AP" album which was released on January 15th, 2013 shared by the designer Joe Perez himself. The sixth page shows one half of the same photo used in the "Wild for the Night" single art with much less editing. In the top left corner of this page, in fashion of government documents, shows a declassified stamp which did not appear in the vinyl version of this photo. The seventh page is a continuation of the photo and shows Rocky leaned against a wall.

Name: LONG.LIVE.A$AP (Deluxe) [CD Booklet Page #9 & #10]
Notes: The ninth and tenth pages to the CD booklet for the "LONG.LIVE.A$AP" album which was released on January 15th, 2013 shared by the designer Joe Perez himself. The ninth page shows four members of the A$AP Mob with their heads as skeletons. The four members on this page, from right to left, are A$AP Rocky, A$AP Yams, A$AP Bari and A$AP Ferg. The tenth page of the booklet continues this trend and shows A$AP Ty Beats, A$AP Nast, A$AP Twelvyy, who for some reason only has one y listed in his name, and A$AP Ant. These pages as does the rest of the booklet feature a style of government documents as the tenth page even has a declassified stamp.

Name: LONG.LIVE.A$AP (Deluxe) [CD Booklet Page #11 & #12]
Notes: The eleventh and twelfth pages to the CD booklet for the "LONG.LIVE.A$AP" album which was released on January 15th, 2013 shared by the designer Joe Perez himself.

Name: LONG.LIVE.A$AP [Animated Promo Cover]
Notes: Official animated promo cover used for the release of LONG.LIVE.A$AP. Was revealed when the original cover art was also revealed.

Name:
Fashion Killa
(prod. A$AP Rocky, FRIENDZONE & Héctor Delgado)
Notes: The main single cover for the song "Fashion Killa" which was originally released on the "LONG.LIVE.A$AP" album on January 15th, 2013 , but was released as a single on July 1st, 2013. This is the most basic of the single arts for "LONG.LIVE.A$AP" as it features a white background and the song title on a black streak similar to the main album cover.

Name:
Fashion Killa [Alternate Cover]
(prod. A$AP Rocky, FRIENDZONE & Héctor Delgado)
Notes: An alternate single art for the song "Fashion Killa" which was originally released on the "LONG.LIVE.A$AP" album on January 15th, 2013 , but was released as a single on July 1st, 2013. This single art was created by Joe Perez and resembles other single arts for the album, "Long Live A$AP" and "Wild for the Night" with similar themes such as the throne Rocky sits in.

Name:
Beauty and the Beast: Slowed Down Sessions, Chapter 1
(as Lord Flacko)
(BEAUTY N THE BEA$T - SDSCH. 1, BEAUTY N DA BEAST: SLOWED DOWN SESSIONS - CHAPTER .1, SDSCH. 1)

Era: Beauty and the Beast: Slowed Down Sessions, Chapter 1
Name: Beauty And The Beast: Slowed Down Sessions, Chapter 1
Notes: The main mixtape art for "Beauty And The Beast: Slowed Down Sessions, Chapter 1". The cover was first shown in the YouTube upload for the song "Unicorn"  titled ""UNICORN" (FEB. 14 2013) - LORD FLVCKO - SDSCH. 1" which was uploaded on March 3rd, 2014. The video zoomed into several parts of the cover while the song played. The designer of the cover Rud Cef posted the original artwork on March 6th, 2014 along with a alternate cover he designed. The cover shows a drawing of Rocky holding some sort of beast-like baby creature with a shadowy figure behind him. In the door frame a woman can be seen fitting the theme of beauty and the beast. Other small details to note is the poster on the wall is a recreation of a very famous picture of Rocky that once blew up on A$AP Yams Tumblr in 2009 or 2010. Along with this a candelabra can be seen likely referring to the Disney movie "Beauty And The Beast" itself.

Era: Beauty and the Beast: Slowed Down Sessions, Chapter 1
Name: Beauty And The Beast: Slowed Down Sessions, Chapter 1 [Alternate Cover]

Era: Beauty and the Beast: Slowed Down Sessions, Chapter 1
Name: Beauty And The Beast: Slowed Down Sessions, Chapter 1 [Alt Cover 2]

Era: Beauty and the Beast: Slowed Down Sessions, Chapter 1
Name: Beauty And The Beast: Slowed Down Sessions, Chapter 2

Trillmatic
(with A$AP Nast & Method Man) (prod. A$AP Ty Beats)
Notes: Cover art for the A$AP Mob single 'Trillmatic' with A$AP Nast & Method Man.

Name:
Trillmatic [Alternate Cover]
(with A$AP Nast & Method Man) (prod. A$AP Ty Beats)
Notes: Alternative cover art for the A$AP Mob single 'Trillmatic' with A$AP Nast & Method Man.

Name:
See Me
(with A$AP Ant) (prod. Davon Productions)
Notes: Cover art for the A$AP Mob single 'See Me' with A$AP Ant.

Name:
Xscape
(with A$AP Twelvyy) (prod. Tay Soprano)
Notes: Cover art for the A$AP Mob single 'Xscape' with A$AP Twelvyy.

Name:

Name: TESTING
Notes: Cover art for A$AP Rocky's third studio album "TESTING". The cover art features “dummies” all styled very similarly to Rocky, with his iconic French braids. The photo taken for the cover was taken by Cary Fagan and was designed by Moonbase. The art is a direct reference to Smif-N-Wessun's 1995 album "Dah Shinin'". The title to that album most likely influenced the name for "Praise The Lord (Da Shine)" aswell. The cover is often misinterpreted as many people believe it to be referencing N.W.A.'s 1988 album "Straight Outta Compton" rather than "Dah Shinin".

Name:
Tailor Swif [V1]
(prod. Hitkidd)
Notes: Presumably earlier single art for the leaked track "Taylor Swift", also known as "Wetty". The cover was posted by Rocky in late 2022 / early 2023 and was deleted shortly after. Merch with the cover was put out in early 2023 as well but the song never got an official release, and as Rocky stated in a Complex interview it won't be on his album "DON'T BE DUMB". Follows the film-like theme from many previous singles "Babushka Boi", "D.M.B." and "Shittin' Me".
